original_title: Field Study in Deploying Restless Multi-Armed Bandits — Assisting Non-Profits in Improving Maternal and Child Health

structural_core.decision_variables: Binary per-farmer per-month decision: place an advisor follow-up call this month, yes/no. Selected subset is at most m of the N enrolled farmers.
structural_core.objective: Maximise cumulative farmer engagement with the monthly voice advisory (approximately, total farmer-months in the 'engaging' state) across a multi-month horizon, under a fixed per-month follow-up-call budget.
structural_core.constraints: Hard per-period capacity: at most ~400 advisor follow-up calls per month across N~28,000 enrolled farmers (N >> m). Advisors are a fixed, shared resource; decisions must refresh each month.
structural_core.dynamics: Each farmer's engagement evolves as a hidden stochastic process over discrete monthly time-steps, influenced by whether an advisor call was placed. Transitions differ per farmer (latent) and are influenced by intervention vs non-intervention. Historical trajectories available but were generated under the incumbent rotation policy, not random assignment.
structural_core.information_structure: Static per-farmer features (district, village, language, age band, farm size, crop mix, education, phone type, distance to office, collective membership) known a priori. Monthly engagement (listen duration) observed per farmer. Per-farmer intervention-response parameters are unknown and must be inferred from historical data.
structural_core.data: Five years of monthly engagement logs per farmer (listen/no-listen, seconds listened, call placed yes/no); advisor call logs with structured notes; farmer enrolment attributes; district-level seasonal context (rainfall, market prices). Historical trajectories are from a deployed rule-based rotation policy — not a randomised policy.
structural_core.scale: N ~ 28,000 enrolled farmers; m ~ 400 follow-up calls per month; T ~ 6-8 month pilot horizon (and ongoing multi-year programme thereafter); 12 human advisors; three districts; ~5 years of historical monthly logs.

domain_selection_rationale: Agricultural extension / smallholder-farmer advisory retention preserves every structural invariant of the source paper (per-entity hidden engagement state, per-period binary intervention, tight shared-staff capacity, cumulative engagement objective, historical logs generated by an incumbent rule-based rotation policy, deployment-in-the-field flavour where the incumbent must be beaten) while sitting in a domain (agriculture/rural livelihoods) that is far from ARMMAN/maternal-health vocabulary and from the other canonical RMAB public-health targets (TB adherence, HIV, homeless shelter, lead paint, poaching). Monthly cadence deliberately differs from paper_04's weekly home-visit rhythm. Parole check-ins were a strong alternative but carry a criminal-justice political charge and have some overlap with well-known sequential-decision literature in that space. Adult literacy retention was plausible but the 'capacity' story (who makes the nudge calls, at what cost) is thinner and less grounded.

leakage_queries.query: smallholder farmer advisory voice message engagement retention
leakage_queries.assessment: Surfaces agricultural-extension, ICT4D, and voice-IVR-for-farmers literature (e.g., Precision Development / Digital Green / Avaaj Otalo). ARMMAN / maternal-health RMAB papers are not in the top results because the domain vocabulary pulls Google firmly into agriculture. Low leakage risk.

leakage_queries.query: how to prioritise limited staff follow-up calls across thousands of beneficiaries monthly
leakage_queries.assessment: Returns generic NGO operations, CRM, and call-centre prioritisation material; possibly some adherence-monitoring ML papers. The distinctive ARMMAN field-study paper is not plausibly on page one without the words 'maternal' or 'pregnancy'. Low-to-moderate risk; the structural language is generic enough to stay clear of the source.

leakage_queries.query: data-driven approach to monthly outreach call prioritisation pilot evaluation
leakage_queries.assessment: Surfaces marketing / customer-churn and public-health outreach methodology papers. The ARMMAN field-deployment paper is keyword-anchored to 'maternal', 'pregnancy', 'ARMMAN', 'India', none of which appear in the proxy, so it is unlikely to rank on page one. Low leakage risk.

gold_method_family: Sequential resource scheduling under capacity constraints using RMAB / Whittle index
gold_key_method: Real-world RMAB deployment with field validation; significantly outperforms rule-based baselines
